signals.reasoning: BTC remains firm, but the edge is still too mild to justify a fresh direction under a caution regime.
ETH is still the cleanest reduced-size long because price and open interest are rising together while funding stays flat.
SOL still reads as a short-covering rally rather than a durable continuation entry, so I would keep it watch-only.
PENGU has momentum but not enough structural edge or crowding to justify forcing a position in caution.
NOT is firm but still too low-liquidity and too middling mechanically to justify a fresh trade.
BLAST still qualifies as a reduced-size squeeze long because funding is deeply negative while price is up, but poor liquidity keeps conviction capped.
VVV has enough squeeze fuel to avoid shorting, but it is already too extended to chase confidently here.
TON remains the clearest reduced-size peak fade because it is still very extended and funding has turned positive, even if the chart has not fully broken yet.
